Based on my search, here are the best personal analytics dashboard apps for quantified self tracking in 2025:

## Top Dashboard & Aggregation Apps

**Gyroscope** - A Quantified Self health dashboard available on web, iOS, & Android. **Exist** - Tracks everything in one place (web, iOS & Android).(no content)**Flow Dashboard** - A habit tracker and personal data analytics app.(no content)**Welltory** - A health app that lets users track heart rate variability, stress, and energy levels, along with aggregated health and fitness analytics from different wearable devices and apps.(no content)**Conjure** - Offers habits, goals and time tracking with a rules engine, data layer, API, and dashboards (Web, Desktop, iOS, Android).

## Data Aggregation & Integration Tools

**Zenobase** - Stores, aggregates and visualizes your data from multiple 3rd party sources (web).(no content)**Vital** - A Plaid-like widget that joins together all your fitness data, wearables data and at-home lab tests.(no content)**FitnessSyncer** - Joins health and fitness data into a single centralized platform.(no content)**Dogsheep** - A collection of data exporters and tools for personal analytics using SQLite and/or Datasette.

## Open Source Options

**Heedy** - A repository for all of your quantified-self data.(no content)**Fluxtream** - An open-source non-profit personal data visualization framework. **BetterSelf** - An open-sourced app for tracking supplements and habits. **QS Ledger** - Open Source Personal Data Aggregator and Data Analysis built on Python using Jupyter Notebooks.

## 2025 Updates

**Amplenote** announced that Q3 2025 is focused on analytics and action, shipping a completely revamped "Completed Tasks" dashboard that transforms raw data into a personal roadmap for a more productive life.(no content)Their "Good Life Algorithm" is described as "a dashboard you can visit every day to make more informed decisions to improve your long-term wellbeing."

## Platform-Based Options

**Apple Health** - Multi-purpose tracking platform for Apple devices that tracks activities, sleep, nutrition, mindfulness, and other metrics.(no content)**Google Fit** - Open ecosystem to store, access, and manage fitness data (Android).

Here are the best goal-setting and OKR apps for personal objectives in 2025, based on the search results:

## Top OKR & Goal-Setting Apps for 2025

### All-in-One Platforms

**ClickUp Goals**
ClickUp Goals can be used to manage OKRs in a central hub. This feature is a great fit for different goal-setting purposes, from personal OKRs to company-wide objectives.(no content)You can easily customize goals by naming them, setting a due date, adding assignees, controlling access, and sharing a detailed breakdown of each goal into measurable objectives and milestones.

**Asana Goals**
Asana's Goals feature is an organization-wide tool that can help your entire company set, track, and communicate about goals.(no content)This goal-setting methodology allows your team to set and track measurable goals, following a simple formula.

### Dedicated OKR Platforms

**Perdoo**
Perdoo is a goal-setting and OKR platform designed to help businesses of all sizes align objectives, track performance, and achieve their goals. It offers a range of tools for tracking progress, collaboration, and strategic alignment. Perdoo is best for growing companies that need a comprehensive OKR platform with strategic alignment features and real-time tracking capabilities.

**Quantive**
Quantive helps you create multi-level OKRs, view dynamic OKR dashboard insights, assign OKR ownership, and create custom Insightboards. These can be tracked and kept visible using over 170 integrations.

**Profit.co**
Profit.co offers a clean, straightforward platform for managing OKRs, tasks, and performance metrics. It's designed to keep goal-tracking simple and effective by combining goal-setting and task management in one place.(no content)It's free for up to 5 users, then $9/user/month for the Growth plan.

**SugarOKR**
SugarOKR is an easy-to-use OKR platform designed to help startups and growing teams track their objectives. It's ideal for startups and small to medium-sized businesses looking for an easy-to-use OKR platform without overly complex features.

**SimpleOKR**
SimpleOKR has simple pricing—one price of $49.99 per month with unlimited users. It offers a free 7-day trial for new users.

### For Personal Goal Tracking

**Hive**
Hive is one of the few apps that blends goal tracking with robust project management features. It gives you the flexibility to structure your goals using task lists, deadlines, milestones, and progress metrics. Whether you're setting personal habits or managing team objectives, Hive adapts to how you work best.

**HabitNow** (mentioned for personal tracking)
One approach is to make goals become habits so you don't need to rely on willpower or motivation. You can track goals regularly on habit tracker apps like HabitNow.

### Key Tips for Personal OKR Success

Christina Wodtke from Stanford suggests writing OKRs such that you have a 50% confidence level that you can achieve them.(no content)Quarterly OKRs strike the perfect balance between long-term vision and short-term execution. A three-month cycle offers enough time to make meaningful progress while staying flexible enough to adapt to unexpected challenges.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Here are the best mood tracker and mental health apps for anxiety and depression in 2025 based on my search:

## Top Recommended Apps

### Best Overall Mood Tracking
- **Daylio Journal** - Tops the list for 2025 due to its intuitive interface, analytics, and proven emotional tracking success.(no content)Daylio doesn't ask you to type paragraphs—you just tap how you feel. It combines mood tracking, habit logging, and visual analytics.

- **Bearable** - Chosen as the best app for mood and symptom tracking because it strikes the perfect balance between ease of use and in-depth tracking.(no content)Bearable offers highly customizable tracking for mood, sleep, habits, energy, and symptoms. It's perfect for clients with complex mental health needs or co-occurring physical health conditions.

### Best for Anxiety
- **Worry Watch** - Best for tracking anxiety triggers.(no content)Worry Watch is like a personal journal for your anxious thoughts, helping you track, analyze, and challenge them over time.

- **Sanvello** - Best for Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) on the go. Sanvello offers tools based in CBT, mindfulness, and mood tracking, with access to guided paths, community discussion boards, and coping strategies to manage anxiety, stress, and depression. It's one of the few mental health apps with peer support AND clinical backing.

### Best for Depression
- **eMoods** - A user-friendly app for patients to track symptom data relating to Bipolar I and II disorders, Depression, PTSD, and Anxiety Disorders. It helps identify triggers and patterns to help prevent relapses, and enhance doctor's visits with detailed data exports.

- **MoodTools** - A simple mental health app for those struggling with depression. MoodTools costs $1.99 monthly or $29.99 annually.

### Best AI-Powered Support
- **Wysa** - An AI-powered app that offers 24/7 support through a friendly chatbot companion. The app helps users manage stress and mental health conditions like anxiety and depression using evidence-based techniques like cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and mindfulness exercises.

- **Youper** - An innovative mental health app that uses an AI-powered chatbot to guide users through CBT exercises, helping manage challenges like anxiety, depression, and self-confidence.(no content)Youper costs $69.99 annually, with a 7-day free trial.

### Additional Notable Apps
- **MindDoc** - A mental health app for self-monitoring and emotional well-being. It helps users track their mood, emotions, and symptoms over time through daily check-ins and guided reflections, with educational content on mental health topics, mindfulness exercises, and CBT tools.

- **T2 Mood Tracker** - Developed for clinical use, provides comprehensive mood tracking across six key mental health areas including anxiety, stress, depression, PTSD, and general wellbeing.

### Free Option
iPhone and iPad have mood tracking features built into the standard Health app. You can open your system settings and tap Mental Wellbeing then State of Mind to track your mental wellbeing by logging your moods and emotions and taking standardized mental health assessments. Free on iOS devices.

## Important Note
Research shows that evidence-based emotional wellness apps can significantly reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression when used consistently. However, they work best as supplements to professional therapy rather than replacements, especially for severe mental health conditions.

Here are the best mood tracker and mental health apps for anxiety and depression in 2025 based on my search:

## Top Recommended Apps

### Best Overall Mood Tracking
- **Daylio Journal** - Tops the list for 2025 due to its intuitive interface, analytics, and proven emotional tracking success.(no content)Daylio doesn't ask you to type paragraphs—you just tap how you feel. It combines mood tracking, habit logging, and visual analytics.

- **Bearable** - Chosen as the best app for mood and symptom tracking because it strikes the perfect balance between ease of use and in-depth tracking.(no content)Bearable offers highly customizable tracking for mood, sleep, habits, energy, and symptoms. It's perfect for clients with complex mental health needs or co-occurring physical health conditions.

### Best for Anxiety
- **Worry Watch** - Best for tracking anxiety triggers.(no content)Worry Watch is like a personal journal for your anxious thoughts, helping you track, analyze, and challenge them over time.

- **Sanvello** - Best for Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) on the go. Sanvello offers tools based in CBT, mindfulness, and mood tracking, with access to guided paths, community discussion boards, and coping strategies to manage anxiety, stress, and depression. It's one of the few mental health apps with peer support AND clinical backing.

### Best for Depression
- **eMoods** - A user-friendly app for patients to track symptom data relating to Bipolar I and II disorders, Depression, PTSD, and Anxiety Disorders. It helps identify triggers and patterns to help prevent relapses, and enhance doctor's visits with detailed data exports.

- **MoodTools** - A simple mental health app for those struggling with depression. MoodTools costs $1.99 monthly or $29.99 annually.

### Best AI-Powered Support
- **Wysa** - An AI-powered app that offers 24/7 support through a friendly chatbot companion. The app helps users manage stress and mental health conditions like anxiety and depression using evidence-based techniques like cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and mindfulness exercises.

- **Youper** - An innovative mental health app that uses an AI-powered chatbot to guide users through CBT exercises, helping manage challenges like anxiety, depression, and self-confidence.(no content)Youper costs $69.99 annually, with a 7-day free trial.

### Additional Notable Apps
- **MindDoc** - A mental health app for self-monitoring and emotional well-being. It helps users track their mood, emotions, and symptoms over time through daily check-ins and guided reflections, with educational content on mental health topics, mindfulness exercises, and CBT tools.

- **T2 Mood Tracker** - Developed for clinical use, provides comprehensive mood tracking across six key mental health areas including anxiety, stress, depression, PTSD, and general wellbeing.

### Free Option
iPhone and iPad have mood tracking features built into the standard Health app. You can open your system settings and tap Mental Wellbeing then State of Mind to track your mental wellbeing by logging your moods and emotions and taking standardized mental health assessments. Free on iOS devices.

## Important Note
Research shows that evidence-based emotional wellness apps can significantly reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression when used consistently. However, they work best as supplements to professional therapy rather than replacements, especially for severe mental health conditions.

A Comparison","url":"https://bookriot.com/best-book-tracking-app/"},{"title":"Goodbye, Goodreads: Five New Reading Tracker Apps to Try","url":"https://bookriot.com/new-reading-tracker-apps-to-try/"}]

Here's what I found on the best book tracking and reading log apps as Goodreads alternatives in 2025:

## Top Goodreads Alternatives for 2025

### The StoryGraph
"The StoryGraph is the platform most poised to be a Goodreads-killer. It has all of the same benefits and features of Goodreads — just better."(no content)StoryGraph is the go-to choice for readers who love data. It provides detailed analytics about your habits: moods, pacing, genres, and more, and delivers tailored recommendations based on what you've read.(no content)Books are user-reviewed by mood, pace, character development, diversity level, and there's even optional content warning labels. You can also import your Goodreads library automatically.

### Bookly
Bookly gamifies reading. It times your sessions, encourages you to set daily goals, and rewards you with badges for consistency.(no content)The app offers great note-taking and timer features, sends motivational notifications, uses an achievement system and challenges (the Bookly Readathons) to motivate readers even more, and also has ambient sounds to accompany reading.

### Bookmory
According to Book Riot, Bookmory is "the best book tracking app I've tried." It lets you keep track of how many books you've read and how long you've read them, save quotes, take notes, share reading accomplishments, see reading statistics, and much more.

### Hardcover
Founded in 2021 as an Amazon-free alternative to Goodreads, Hardcover blends social discovery and detailed book tracking. It's crafted by a small, independent team and remains ad-free. You can track reading progress, ratings, and notes, discover trending books, and connect with other readers.(no content)It offers unlimited entries, progress tracking, half-star ratings, custom lists, and privacy controls per book. It also supports imports from major platforms like Goodreads and StoryGraph.

### Libib
Libib offers visually appealing library organization, an option to sync your collection across all devices with Cloud Sync, an extensive book database, and cool data analytics. You can also catalog board games, movies, music, and video games.(no content)A free version offers up to 5,000 items and up to 100 collections.

### Litsy
Available on phone and desktop, Litsy is "almost like Instagram meets Goodreads." You can post pictures, and people can like/reply via their feed or through searching for specific books.

### PageBound (Emerging)
PageBound is pitched as "Goodreads meets Reddit," with a social focus. It's a newer option that's been gaining attention on social media.

## Why People Are Leaving Goodreads
Goodreads has been reduced to a legacy app. Whether it's the website, the iOS, or the iPadOS app — all of them have an outdated User Interface and Experience with bare minimum book logging features and a not-so-great book rating system.(no content)Goodreads has been owned by Amazon since 2015, which is a problem for anyone concerned about handing over their data to Amazon or for anyone working to completely divest from Amazon tools.

## Recommendation
Many readers use a mix of apps — logging public reviews on Goodreads, getting mood recs on StoryGraph, and setting daily goals in Bookly. You don't need to pick just one — find the combo that works for your reading life.

Here are the best browser productivity extensions for focus in 2025 based on my search:

## Top Focus & Distraction-Blocking Extensions

**StayFocusd**
StayFocusd is a powerful tool designed to help users control their time online by restricting access to distracting websites. It allows you to set daily limits on time-wasting sites, helping you maintain focus and productivity.(no content)It blocks entire websites or specific pages, allows setting time limits for social media and other distractions, and includes a "Nuclear Option" to block access to sites completely for a set time.

**Forest**
Forest takes the Pomodoro technique and adds a twist: every time you stay focused, a tree grows on your screen. Leave the app to scroll or switch tabs, and your tree withers. It's a small but powerful motivator to stay present. This extension is perfect for students, writers, and deep workers who struggle with digital temptation.

**Freedom**
The Freedom app is designed to block distracting websites and apps across all your devices. Whether you're working on your laptop, tablet, or phone, Freedom ensures you stay focused.

**Inbox When Ready**
Inbox When Ready works by hiding your inbox by default, only revealing it when you consciously decide to check your emails, ensuring you only get sidetracked when you truly want to be.

## Productivity Dashboard Extensions

**Momentum**
Momentum is a Chrome extension that transforms your new tab into a personalized dashboard. It offers features like daily inspirational quotes, to-do lists, weather updates, and beautiful background images to help you stay focused and organized.

## Task Management Extensions

**Todoist**
Todoist is a to-do list app with a Chrome extension that speeds up adding tasks. What draws users to Todoist for Chrome is how effortlessly it integrates with the browser, letting you jot down tasks without disrupting workflow. It syncs across all devices in real time.

## Tab Management Extensions

**OneTab**
OneTab consolidates all your open tabs into a single tab, freeing up memory and reducing distractions.

**Tab Wrangler**
Tab Wrangler automatically closes inactive tabs, reducing clutter and improving browser performance. It auto-closes unused tabs after a set time and saves closed tabs for easy recovery.

## Best Practices

Installing too many extensions slows down Chrome and creates complexity. The recommended approach is to stick to 7-10 essential extensions that directly address your pain points.

Based on my search, here are the **best meal planning and nutrition tracking apps for 2025**:

## Top Picks

### **MyFitnessPal** 
A dietitian favorite with a database of over 14 million verified foods that tracks patterns to help you understand how your food choices impact your health, energy, mood, and focus.(no content)The interface is clean and simple, and you can create and save recipes. Pricing: Free to download, or $20/month, or $80/year for premium.

### **Cronometer**
A precision-focused nutrition tracker that tracks up to 84 micronutrients (vitamins, minerals, etc.) in addition to calories and macros. Its food database uses verified sources like the USDA, meaning very accurate data without user-created clutter.(no content)The app is free with ads, and Gold subscription is ~$8.99/month or $49/year.

### **Lifesum**
Provides expert advice on what, when, and how much to eat based on your goals and lifestyle, with in-depth questions and detailed breakdown of protein, carb, and fat intake.(no content)Offers intermittent fasting support with various fasting schedules (16:8, 5:2, etc.) that adjust calorie/macro targets for eating windows.

### **MacroFactor**
Stands out for its adaptive calorie budgeting: it updates your weekly calorie/macro targets based on your actual weight trends and intake—useful when plateaus hit.

### **Fitia**
Combines AI-driven convenience with accurate manual control for sustainable nutrition management over time.(no content)Offers AI-assisted multi-modal logging (voice, photo, text), full manual control for verification, and multi-user pricing making advanced tools accessible to several people under one subscription.

### **Lose It!**
Tracks nutrients and calories while providing an intermittent fasting tracker. Now offers dietitian-curated strategies supporting multiple diet types including low-carb and plant-based.

### **MyNetDiary**
Great for beginners - lets you select a diet that fits your lifestyle (low-carb, keto, vegan, calorie counting) and guides you to set weekly targets and goals with barcode scanning for easy logging.

## Quick Recommendations

| Goal | Best App |
|------|----------|
| Micronutrients tracking | Cronometer |
| Biggest database + social features | MyFitnessPal or Lose It! |
| Intelligent meal planning | Fitia |
| Lifestyle focus with fasting | Lifesum |

Most apps offer free trials, so you can test a few to find your perfect match.

## Top Recommended Apps

### Best Overall
- **SHRED App**: Suitable for both at-home workouts and commercial gyms, the SHRED app is the pick for the best workout app overall because it offers high-quality weight training programs and serves as a virtual personal trainer at a fraction of the cost.(no content)The app receives 5 out of 5 stars for both ease of use and equipment demands, and is very intuitive, making it easy to find your next workout.

- **Strong**: Strong is the most intuitive workout and exercise tracker for any fitness routine, designed to help you get better results from your workouts.(no content)It has a huge wealth of features for building sets and contains a massive library of exercises. - **Hevy**: Hevy is the #1 free gym workout tracker & planner app for iOS and Android, allowing you to plan your weight lifting routines, log workouts, and track your exercise progress.(no content)Hevy helps you stick to a routine with the ability to sort workouts by location and equipment, letting you choose between workouts designed for the gym vs. home.

- **BetterMe**: If you're new to your fitness journey and aren't entirely sure where to start, BetterMe can be a solid option as the best workout app for beginners. With nutrition tracking and a workout library filled with bodyweight and dumbbell workouts, it provides a great place for anyone to start.

- **Gymverse**: Where Gymverse stands out is that the app features integrated video tutorials for each exercise. The videos are clear and well-produced—perfect for people new to strength training.

- **Jefit**: One of the favorite free apps for tracking strength training is Jefit. It allows you to track your own strength training workouts with their library of exercises, each with written and visual instructions.

From there, your Future Coach creates weekly, highly personalized training programs just for you, and adapts them whenever you need. The in-app chat feature allows users to communicate with trainers on the fly.

### Best for Fast Logging

## Key Features to Look For
These apps often offer smart capabilities to track your progress over time and other important workout metrics that can help keep you motivated and accountable.

Based on my search, here are the top workout and fitness tracking apps for gym exercise in 2025:

## Top Recommended Apps

### Best Overall
- **SHRED App**: Suitable for both at-home workouts and commercial gyms, the SHRED app is the pick for the best workout app overall because it offers high-quality weight training programs and serves as a virtual personal trainer at a fraction of the cost.(no content)The app receives 5 out of 5 stars for both ease of use and equipment demands, and is very intuitive, making it easy to find your next workout.

### Best for Strength Training
- **Strong**: Strong is the most intuitive workout and exercise tracker for any fitness routine, designed to help you get better results from your workouts.(no content)It has a huge wealth of features for building sets and contains a massive library of exercises. It also lets you superset, set auto rest timers, and add and categorize your own exercises.

- **Hevy**: Hevy is the #1 free gym workout tracker & planner app for iOS and Android, allowing you to plan your weight lifting routines, log workouts, and track your exercise progress.(no content)Hevy helps you stick to a routine with the ability to sort workouts by location and equipment, letting you choose between workouts designed for the gym vs. home.

### Best for Beginners
- **BetterMe**: If you're new to your fitness journey and aren't entirely sure where to start, BetterMe can be a solid option as the best workout app for beginners. With nutrition tracking and a workout library filled with bodyweight and dumbbell workouts, it provides a great place for anyone to start.

- **Gymverse**: Where Gymverse stands out is that the app features integrated video tutorials for each exercise. The videos are clear and well-produced—perfect for people new to strength training.

### Best Free Options
- **Nike Training Club**: There are fitness apps that have fully functioning features available for free—no subscription needed. Nike Training Club has been completely free since 2020.

- **Jefit**: One of the favorite free apps for tracking strength training is Jefit. It allows you to track your own strength training workouts with their library of exercises, each with written and visual instructions.

### Best for Personal Coaching
- **Future**: You can browse through dozens of certified trainers to choose one that best fits your goals. From there, your Future Coach creates weekly, highly personalized training programs just for you, and adapts them whenever you need. The in-app chat feature allows users to communicate with trainers on the fly.

### Best for Fast Logging
- **Setgraph**: Setgraph stands apart from other workout tracker apps by prioritizing speed above all else. While most fitness tracking apps require multiple taps to record a set, Setgraph's lightning-fast interface lets you log with a single swipe.

## Key Features to Look For
These apps often offer smart capabilities to track your progress over time and other important workout metrics that can help keep you motivated and accountable.

Based on the search results, here are the **best social media scheduling and management apps for 2025**:

## Top All-in-One Solutions

**Sprout Social**
Sprout Social is a premium, all-in-one social media management suite built for businesses that take their social strategy seriously.(no content)Where Sprout Social truly shines is in its data and collaboration features. Its reporting capabilities are among the best in the industry, providing beautiful, stakeholder-ready reports on everything from post-performance to competitive analysis.

**Hootsuite**
Hootsuite is one of the OG players in the social media management space. This platform goes far beyond basic scheduling to offer a comprehensive suite for large teams and enterprises needing deep control over their social presence. Hootsuite provides a 360-degree view, combining scheduling with robust analytics, social listening, and an all-in-one engagement inbox.

## Best for Agencies

**Sendible**
Sendible is the most scalable social media scheduler and management tool, which makes it a great choice for growing agencies that want to keep costs to a minimum. It's also affordable for individuals.(no content)Sendible sets itself apart because it also offers white-labeling options, allowing agencies to brand the platform with their own logos and colors.

**Agorapulse**
Agorapulse is a powerful social media tool that's well suited to agencies & social media managers. Over 3,000 agencies use Agorapulse thanks to its collaboration features and advanced reporting capabilities.(no content)Paid plans start from $69/month per user; save up to 30% with annual billing.

## Best for Small Businesses & Creators

**Buffer**
Buffer has long been a favorite among small businesses and creators because it's clean, intuitive, and gets the job done without overwhelming you. Where enterprise tools can feel bloated with features you'll never touch, Buffer focuses on a streamlined scheduling experience.(no content)It now supports Facebook, X, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Mastodon, Bluesky, Threads, Pinterest, and Google Business Profile.

**SocialBee**
SocialBee is a powerful all-in-one social media management platform designed for small business owners and marketers. It simplifies content creation, post scheduling, and performance analytics by combining AI-powered features with an intuitive, unified dashboard.(no content)Paid plans start at $29 per month.

## Best for Instagram-Focused Users

**Pallyy**
Pallyy lets you drag media onto the planning grid, rearrange them, add captions, and schedule or share with your team for approval. It also lets you plan and schedule other Instagram content, like Reels, Stories, and Carousels.(no content)Given how good it is, Pallyy is surprisingly affordable. It offers one of the most generous free plans you'll find anywhere.

**Later**
Later stands out for its Visual Feed Planner, to curate the aesthetic of your feed before publishing. You can drag and drop photos and videos to rearrange the order, giving you a clear picture of how your posts will appear to followers on their feeds.

## Best Budget Options

**Metricool**
Metricool is an affordable social media scheduling tool with a great free plan. Use it to schedule posts to Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Pinterest, YouTube, TikTok, and more from one location.

**Publer**
Publer offers a link in bio tool and plenty of analytics to help you measure your campaign performance.(no content)Free plan available. Paid plans start from $12/month.

## Other Notable Mentions

- **CoSchedule** - CoSchedule's Social Calendar is a complete solution for managing social media and marketing campaigns. It provides a centralized view of all your social media campaigns to spot scheduling gaps, drag and drop posts for rescheduling, and group and organize campaigns.

- **SocialPilot** - Manage the social calendar with 500+ posts scheduled at once.

- **HopperHQ** - HopperHQ has one of the best user interfaces of any social media scheduling tool. There's only one plan and it includes unlimited posts, unlimited users, and access to all features.

## Best Video Editing Apps for Content Creators in 2025

Based on my search, here are the top video editing apps recommended for content creators in 2025:

### Professional/Desktop Options

- **Adobe Premiere Pro** is a professional-grade video editing software widely recognized as the industry standard. It offers a comprehensive suite of tools perfect for crafting high-quality social media content, and its robust feature set also makes it suitable for larger productions, film, and television.

- **DaVinci Resolve** stands out as a powerful option for professional-grade features without a hefty price tag. While known in the film industry for its advanced color correction capabilities, its robust free version makes it surprisingly accessible for social media content creators.

- **Final Cut Pro** is a powerful video editing software created by Apple, specifically designed for macOS. Its intuitive interface, combined with robust features and optimization for Apple hardware, allows for a smooth and efficient workflow. It offers a unique magnetic timeline, advanced color correction tools, and is optimized for Apple Silicon, leading to incredibly fast render times.

### Mobile & Short-Form Content

- **CapCut** is the best for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts due to its mobile and desktop compatibility, effects, and AI-powered features.

- **LumaFusion** is one of the most powerful and advanced mobile editing apps available that offers a professional-level experience for both Android and iOS devices.

- **Adobe Premiere Rush** was designed for creators who want professional results but don't need every advanced feature. It's lightweight, easy to use, and works across devices.

### Beginner-Friendly Options

- **iMovie** remains one of the easiest editing tools for beginners. It's free with every Mac and iOS device, offering a clean interface and essential editing features without overwhelming users.

- **Wondershare Filmora** is a great choice if you want to make your videos pop with eye-catching effects but don't want the learning curve that comes with a pro-level editing suite.

### Quick Summary by Use Case

The best video editing software in 2025 depends on your needs: Adobe Premiere Pro is ideal for professionals, DaVinci Resolve for color grading, Final Cut Pro for Mac users, and CapCut for social media creators.

Based on the search results, here are the best form builder and survey apps for 2025:

## Top Form Builder & Survey Apps in 2025

### Free Options

**Google Forms**
Google Forms is considered one of the best free-form and survey apps because it's really easy to start.(no content)It's completely free for anyone with a Google account and offers an intuitive, minimal interface that anyone can learn in minutes.(no content)Your form can also connect directly with a Google Sheet, making it a good choice for those already familiar with Google Workspace.

### Popular Premium Options

**Jotform**
Jotform lets you create forms and surveys for free with a drag-and-drop form builder, allowing you to collect registrations, applications, orders, and payments.(no content)It has an extremely easy drag-and-drop builder with high-quality templates, and offers an exceptionally clean and attractive user interface—possibly the smoothest, least clunky experience of all online survey tools.

**Typeform**

**SurveyMonkey**

### Other Notable Options

**Paperform**

**Wufoo**
Wufoo is one of the oldest form and survey apps with helpful features including payment processing services and a file uploader for images and documents.

**WPForms** (WordPress)

Based on the search results, here are the **best photo management and organization apps for personal photos in 2025**:

## Top Picks

### Cloud-Based Options
- **Google Photos** - The stock Android photo manager (also available on iOS) with many great features. It offers 15GB of free storage, which is 10GB more than Apple Photos.(no content)It has an AI feature that makes it easy to organize smartphone photos.

- **Apple Photos** - Free to use but with only 5GB of iCloud storage. You can purchase extra storage: 50GB for 99 cents/month, 200GB for $2.99/month, up to 12TB for $64.99/month.

### Desktop/Professional Solutions
- **Adobe Lightroom** - Considered the best all-round photo organizer and editing software for all types of photographers.

- **Excire Foto 2025** - For those looking to automate organizational workflow with AI, this is a top pick.(no content)It features AI-powered tools including automatic keywording, people & face search, and duplicate search.(no content)However, it does not include photo editing capabilities and has a higher license price.

- **ACDSee Photo Studio Ultimate 2025** - A good alternative to Adobe Lightroom Classic, offering an all-in-one solution for digital asset management and photo editing. Users can organize photos using keywords, date, categories, location, color labels, and EXIF data.

- **Adobe Bridge** - A free photo organizing software. Photographers can use it to browse images, rank them, and view metadata.

### Privacy-Focused Options
- **Mylio Photos** - The only photo manager where you can edit, organize, sync and protect a lifetime of photos and videos while keeping files private on your devices.(no content)It organizes scattered photos from devices, cloud accounts, and hard drives into one library. Unlike typical apps, it doesn't rely solely on the cloud—you decide where to store your photos.

### iOS-Specific Apps
- **Utiful** - Unlike Apple Photos and Google Photos, this app lets you move (not just copy) photos out of your camera roll and sort them by category into separate folders. It's the only app that lets you strictly separate different categories into folders and subfolders.

- **Clever Cleaner** - One of the best cleaning apps on the App Store. It's completely free with zero ads or in-app purchases, and automatically compiles similar photos, screenshots, Live Photos, and large files for review.

- **Excire Foto 2025** - For those looking to automate organizational workflow with AI, this is a top pick.(no content)It features AI-powered tools including automatic keywording, people & face search, and duplicate search.(no content)However, it does not include photo editing capabilities and has a higher license price.

- **ACDSee Photo Studio Ultimate 2025** - A good alternative to Adobe Lightroom Classic, offering an all-in-one solution for digital asset management and photo editing. Users can organize photos using keywords, date, categories, location, color labels, and EXIF data.

- **Adobe Bridge** - A free photo organizing software. Photographers can use it to browse images, rank them, and view metadata.

### Privacy-Focused Options
- **Mylio Photos** - The only photo manager where you can edit, organize, sync and protect a lifetime of photos and videos while keeping files private on your devices.(no content)It organizes scattered photos from devices, cloud accounts, and hard drives into one library. Unlike typical apps, it doesn't rely solely on the cloud—you decide where to store your photos.

### iOS-Specific Apps
- **Utiful** - Unlike Apple Photos and Google Photos, this app lets you move (not just copy) photos out of your camera roll and sort them by category into separate folders. It's the only app that lets you strictly separate different categories into folders and subfolders.

- **Clever Cleaner** - One of the best cleaning apps on the App Store. It's completely free with zero ads or in-app purchases, and automatically compiles similar photos, screenshots, Live Photos, and large files for review.

Based on the search results, here are the **best presentation apps and PowerPoint alternatives for 2025**:

## Top PowerPoint Alternatives for 2025

### Free Options

1. **Google Slides** - Best for teams that need seamless collaboration and cloud-first access. Google Slides integrates tightly with Google Workspace and offers reliable real-time editing across devices.

2. **LibreOffice Impress** - LibreOffice Impress is about as close as it gets to simply replicating Microsoft PowerPoint for free.(no content)The software can import files from Keynote, plus there are hundreds of free templates you can download. There's no limit on what fonts you can use.

3. **Canva** - Canva and Google Slides offer mobile apps that can replace PowerPoint on your phone with ease.

### Creative & Dynamic Presentations

4. **Prezi** - Prezi's presentation software is a true alternative to PowerPoint—a presentation platform centered around a zoomable, non-linear canvas, enabling more interactive and engaging storytelling.(no content)Prezi has added new features including a built-in AI tool that can help you build a rough draft of your presentation in minutes.

5. **Gamma** - Gamma is a modern document, webpage, and presentation tool, blending the format of slide decks with interactive elements, such as embeds and carousels. Their AI allows users to add prompts and modify elements of design and copy.

### Professional & AI-Powered

6. **Visme** - Visme is an AI-powered interactive design platform for creating engaging, high-converting pitch decks and other professional branded content quickly. You can access and combine presentation, infographic, and video creation in a single platform.

7. **Beautiful.ai** - Similar to Beautiful.ai, Slidebean's user-friendly PowerPoint alternative software uses artificial intelligence to assist in creating a professional-level visual presentation design.

8. **Slidebean** - Slidebean is a user-friendly PowerPoint alternative software for building pitch decks with AI. Its collaboration and analytics tools let you invite teammates to work with you and track every slide activity.

### For Apple Users

9. **Keynote** - Apple's Keynote remains one of the top presentation programs, offering powerful tools and effects. Keynote offers real-time collaboration and has a simple, intuitive interface.

### Specialized Options

10. **Zoho Show** - Users can create, edit, share and deliver presentations from anywhere. Zoho Show puts a strong emphasis on real-time collaboration and integration with Zoho's suite of business tools.

11. **Vyond** - Vyond is a cloud-based platform that enables users to make animated videos for various purposes, including business presentations, educational content, training materials, and marketing videos.

12. **Animaker Deck** - Animaker Deck lets you get creative with designs, bringing life into your presentations with avatars, interesting GIFs, and animate all elements in one click.

### Key Trend for 2025
The newest crop of online presentation tools go way beyond the classic slideshow experience, with new features to keep your audience's attention, streamline the creation process with AI, and turn slide decks into videos and interactive conversations.

Links: [{"title":"The Best Duolingo Alternatives to Try in 2025 | Copycat Cafe","url":"https://copycatcafe.com/blog/duolingo-alternatives"},{"title":"5 apps you should use instead of Duolingo","url":"https://www.androidauthority.com/duolingo-alternatives-3608570/"},{"title":"The Best Duolingo Alternatives: Fun, Gamified Language Apps","url":"https://www.alllanguageresources.com/duolingo-alternatives/"},{"title":"5 Best Duolingo Alternatives for Free Language Learning | Toolsmart Blog","url":"https://www.toolsmart.ai/blog/5-best-duolingo-alternatives-for-free-language-learning/"},{"title":"The Best Language Learning Apps Ranked: Duolingo, Memrise, Babbel, Busuu Etc.","url":"https://www.olesentuition.co.uk/single-post/which-app-should-i-use-to-learn-a-language-duolingo-memrise-babbel-quizlet-busuu"},{"title":"Top Duolingo Competitors 2025","url":"https://www.g2.com/products/duolingo/competitors/alternatives"},{"title":"Top 10 Best Duolingo Alternatives (November 2025) - UPDATED » Lingoly.io","url":"https://lingoly.io/duolingo-alternatives/"},{"title":"The Best Duolingo Alternatives to Try in 2025 | French Together","url":"https://frenchtogether.com/blog/duolingo-alternatives"},{"title":"5 Duolingo alternatives for next-level foreign language learning","url":"https://www.androidpolice.com/duolingo-alternatives-for-foreign-language-learners/"},{"title":"Best Duolingo Alternatives: Top Language Learning Tools in 2025 | AlternativeTo","url":"https://alternativeto.net/software/duolingo/"}]

Here are the best language learning apps as Duolingo alternatives in 2025:

## Top Duolingo Alternatives

### **Memrise**
The best Duolingo alternative is Memrise, which is free.(no content)Memrise differentiates itself from Duolingo by emphasizing video-based learning featuring native speakers. Unlike Duolingo, which relies on AI-generated sentences and gamified exercises, Memrise helps learners absorb new languages through real-world speech and context-based practice.(no content)One of the benefits of Memrise is the inclusion of native speakers pronouncing the words you're using, and the app focuses more on modern phrases and slang rather than the formal approach Duolingo takes.

### **Babbel**
Babbel is often seen as the "serious" alternative to Duolingo. It's a premium app (subscription-based) that offers expertly designed courses in German (and 13 other languages). From the get-go, Babbel feels more like a structured class: each lesson is 10–15 minutes and walks you through new vocabulary, a dialogue, and then explicitly teaches a grammar point.(no content)One of Babbel's standout features is its specialized courses for travel, business, and culture. The app supports 14 languages, and the first lesson is free. After that, a subscription costs $18 per month, with discounts available for longer-term plans.

### **Busuu**
If you're looking for a more full-fledged Duolingo alternative, Busuu is a free option that supports a wide range of languages. One of the big benefits is that it doesn't limit how much you can learn per day or penalize you for mistakes. The app's courses are also built according to the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR).(no content)What really makes this app stand out is the community feedback. You can upload recordings and written answers to prompts, and other learners will give you tips and corrections.

### **LingoDeer**
LingoDeer was a favorite among alternatives thanks to the availability of a variety of languages (including Mandarin), as well as helpful features such as practicing writing characters and grammar lessons. It also has a character drill feature that really lets you focus on the written language. LingoDeer offers plenty of Asian languages among its offerings.

### **Drops**
Drops is a unique language-learning app with five-minute game-like sessions to teach new words. It has limitations so that the user remains fully focused, which helps improve memorization.(no content)Drops is one of the free apps you can use to learn something new each day. It focuses on vocabulary, so it's a great tool if you have foundational knowledge of a language but want to expand it.

### **Ling**
Ling is one of those language learning apps that feels super approachable and fun, but also really effective. It blends gamification with practical, bite-sized lessons.(no content)Ling offers a huge variety of languages to choose from, and the app features a speaking activity that allows users to record their voice while following the audio, and it evaluates pronunciation.

### **Other Notable Options**
- Rosetta Stone, Busuu, and Lingvist are also considered among the best Duolingo alternatives.
- iTalki is a platform where you can find language tutors online. The tutors can be hired hourly for $6 or $20.
- AnkiDroid is the most popular Open Source alternative to Duolingo.

## Key Takeaway
No single app is a magic bullet to fluency – each has unique strengths. The best app for you depends on your learning style and goals.

Based on the search results, here's an overview of the best online learning course platforms and apps for 2025:

## Market Overview
In 2025, the online education market is projected to reach $185.20 billion, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 8.56% from 2024 to 2029.(no content)Notably, 93% of businesses worldwide are planning to adopt eLearning in 2025, and online learning requires 40% to 60% less time than traditional learning.

## Top Platforms for Learners

### **Coursera**
Coursera has partnered with world-class universities and companies, bringing structured education to anyone with an internet connection. It feels closest to a traditional online university but with flexibility and affordability.(no content)It provides a wide range of online courses and degree programs, including bachelor's and master's degrees, with over 5,000 university courses, partnerships with more than 200 businesses and universities, and a community of over 77 million learners.

### **Udemy**
Udemy is one of the most popular online learning platforms today, with more than 40 million students.(no content)With an affordable pay-per-course model and a user-friendly platform constantly updated with new content, however, the quality of courses can vary significantly.

### **edX**
edX offers a library of courses from top universities like Harvard and MIT. It's perfect for learners who want formal education without the high cost of in-person programs, with most courses being STEM-focused but options across various subjects.(no content)Free classes do not offer certificates; courses with certificates cost between $50 and $300, while full degree programs are usually $1,000 or higher.

### **LinkedIn Learning**
LinkedIn Learning is rated as the best online learning platform for business, with excellent quality control to ensure instructors are subject matter experts with exceptional teaching skills.

### **Skillshare**
Skillshare is great for learning creative skills in a flexible, accessible way. Most courses are project-based and led by real-world creatives, making it a favorite for artists, designers, and makers.(no content)Pricing depends on location, but on average, expect to pay $168 per year or $14 per month.

### **Khan Academy**
Khan Academy is a non-profit offering free online courses and resources, primarily in mathematics and science, with widespread recognition for its comprehensive K-12 curriculum and adaptive learning technology.

### **MasterClass**
MasterClass is an exclusive online learning platform renowned for its celebrity-led courses, covering a broad spectrum of topics including arts, business, and lifestyle, though it may have limited interactivity.

## Top Platforms for Course Creators

### **Teachable**
Teachable is the go-to platform for those looking to sell online courses, coaching, or digital products under their own brand, with over 100,000 creators using it to build and scale their businesses.

### **Thinkific**
Thinkific is an excellent choice for those who want more control over branding and course design without technical headaches, popular among entrepreneurs, business owners, and educators.(no content)Unlike Teachable, Thinkific doesn't take a cut of your revenue.

### **Kajabi**
Kajabi is an all-in-one course platform that includes course hosting, marketing automation, website building, and membership features, though its price makes it better suited for serious course creators and businesses.

### **LearnWorlds**
LearnWorlds is a feature-rich platform that lets instructors create video-based and interactive courses with quizzes, gamification, and certificates, great for educators who want a visually engaging course experience.

### **Circle**
When it comes to building community-based learning experiences, no online course platform does better than Circle.(no content)Circle best suits educators and coaches focused on building interactive, community-based courses.

Based on the search results, here are the top project portfolio management (PPM) apps for enterprise PMOs in 2025:

## Leading Enterprise PPM Solutions

### Top Tier Options

1. **Celoxis**
   - Ideal for large-scale enterprises and complex PMOs managing diverse portfolios, Celoxis excels for teams requiring high flexibility, scalability, and real-time visibility across project statuses and resources.
   - Offers customizable workflows & integrations with 400+ integrations including Jira, Azure DevOps, QuickBooks, and Slack via Zapier. Supports both cloud and on-premise deployments, making it ideal for enterprises with complex governance or data compliance needs.
   - Trusted by Fortune 500 companies & PMOs with 23+ years of experience.

2. **Planview**
   - Their connected platform underpins business and digital transformations of more than 4,500 customers globally, including 59 of the Fortune 100. Planview empowers enterprises to improve time-to-market and predictability, increase efficiency to unlock capacity.

3. **Planisware**
   - Features dynamic portfolio analysis capabilities, which allow managers to categorize, prioritize, and balance projects based on strategic business objectives. This aligns projects directly with corporate strategies. The platform also facilitates portfolio scenario planning with advanced simulation and forecasting tools.
   - A software solutions provider specializing in project portfolio management, designed to bolster product development, engineering, and IT business approaches, with more than two decades of experience.

4. **Adobe Workfront**
   - A cloud-based enterprise work management solution for teams that handle a high volume of projects and requests. Used by teams to plan, assign, and manage work from start through delivery, as well as maintain visibility over resources and priorities.

5. **Smartsheet**
   - Integrates seamlessly with popular business applications, enhancing its functionality.
   - Integrations include DocuSign, Active Directory, LDAP, Jira, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Slack, Box, and Tableau.

### Other Notable Options

Based on the search results, here are the **best travel planning and itinerary apps for 2025**:

## Top Picks

### **TripIt**
Join nearly 20 million travelers on the world's highest-rated travel planner app for trip and itinerary organization.(no content)TripIt takes the chaos out of travel by creating a master itinerary in seconds. Just forward your booking confirmation emails (flights, hotels, car rentals, and more), and TripIt compiles everything into one easy-to-navigate interface.(no content)Find reservation details in a flash with TripIt — even when you're offline. Access this information hands-free with Siri shortcuts!

### **Wanderlog**
Wanderlog is the perfect itinerary/route planner for all types of trips, including road trips. You can create a budget within the app and easily add in stops and explore places of interest near the destinations you plan to visit.(no content)The AI feature, with a Map view allows you to visualize the attraction locations - the optimization feature provides a quick way to plan the day(s). It incorporates Itinerary, booking details, budgeting, search and checklists.(no content)Everything is stored offline so you can access your itinerary when traveling abroad. For road trips, it calculates the time and distance between places and exports them to Google Maps.

### **Hopper**
Hopper is an amazing trip planner app. The app predicts airfares up to 1 year in advance, with 95% accuracy. It analyzes over billions of flight prices and hotels within the day – telling you whether to book your trip now or wait for just a little longer.

### **Google Travel**
Google Travel offers a powerful way to plan a vacation with destination research, trip timelines, and integration with Gmail and Google Calendar. It streamlines research and planning by pulling your reservations into one interface while offering curated suggestions for activities.

### **Sygic Travel Maps**
Sygic Travel Maps is the first travel app to display all of the attractions and places a traveler needs to see and visit on a single map. The app boasts a large database that allows you to find the best hotels, tourist attractions, museums, restaurants, bars, and stores wherever you go.(no content)This mobile app also lets you download offline maps and guides.

## For Group Travel

### **SquadTrip**
SquadTrip is built specifically for group travel and retreats. It makes it easy to manage bookings, payment plans, and trip itineraries. With built-in expense tracking, group chat, and automation for reminders, SquadTrip is highly recommended for anyone managing more than just a casual road trip with friends.

### **Pilot**
Pilot is designed to be flexible, collaborative, and actually fun to use. Whether you're planning solo or with friends, you can build custom itineraries, share and vote on ideas, drop in notes or checklists, and sync bookings automatically.

## For Road Trips

- **Gaia GPS** is a fantastic navigation app which is perfect for hikers and outdoor adventures. It's filled with detailed maps to track hikes, plan trips, and mark spots like campsites or viewpoints along the way. The maps can be used even when offline.

- **Roadtrippers** is recommended for planning road trips.

## Budget-Friendly Options
Skyscanner is ideal for budget travelers or those seeking flexible itineraries, allowing you to find the cheapest dates to travel with a user-friendly interface. Check out Skyscanner's "Explore" feature if you're flexible on destination.
